parentQuestion: A structure that is composed of two or more tissue types that work together to perform specific functions for the body is a(n) ___.

scaffolds.q: What is the organizational hierarchy of the body?
options: Levels of increasing complexity from atoms to organism
scaffolds.explain: The body is organized in LEVELS OF INCREASING COMPLEXITY: Atoms → molecules → organelles → cells → tissues → organs → organ systems → organism.

wrongExplains.0: The body is highly organized, not random.
wrongExplains.2: The hierarchy includes many levels, not just cells and organs.
wrongExplains.3: The body has precise hierarchical organization.

options: The basic units of life — smallest living units that can function independently
scaffolds.explain: CELLS are the BASIC UNITS OF LIFE — the smallest living units that can function independently. All living things are made of cells.

wrongExplains.0: Cells are found in all living things, not just plants.
wrongExplains.2: Cells are made of atoms, but are much larger and living.
wrongExplains.3: Cells are found throughout the body, not just blood.

options: A group of similar cells working together for a common function
scaffolds.explain: A TISSUE is a group of SIMILAR CELLS working together. Muscle tissue = muscle cells. Nervous tissue = nerve cells. Similar cells, common function.

wrongExplains.0: A tissue is made of many cells, not just one.
wrongExplains.2: An organ system is multiple organs; tissue is multiple similar cells.
wrongExplains.3: Tissues are structural units, not nutrients.

scaffolds.q: What are the four primary tissue types?
scaffolds.explain: The four PRIMARY TISSUE TYPES are: EPITHELIAL (covering), CONNECTIVE (support), MUSCLE (movement), and NERVOUS (communication).

wrongExplains.0: These are examples of tissues/organs, not the four primary types.
wrongExplains.2: These are organs, not tissue types.
wrongExplains.3: These are colors, not tissue classifications.

options: Two or more tissue types working together for specific functions
scaffolds.explain: An ORGAN is made of TWO OR MORE TISSUE TYPES working together. The heart has muscle, connective, epithelial, and nervous tissue.

wrongExplains.0: A single tissue type is a tissue, not an organ.
wrongExplains.2: Organs contain multiple cell types organized into tissues.
wrongExplains.3: A body system is multiple organs; an organ is multiple tissues.

options: A group of organs working together for a common purpose
scaffolds.explain: An ORGAN SYSTEM is a group of ORGANS working together. The cardiovascular system includes heart, blood vessels, and blood.

wrongExplains.0: An organ system includes multiple organs, not just one.
wrongExplains.2: A tissue is simpler than an organ system.
wrongExplains.3: Cells make up tissues, which make up organs, which make up organ systems.

scaffolds.q: What is the correct sequence in the organizational hierarchy?
options: Cells → tissues → organs → organ systems → organism
scaffolds.explain: The hierarchy is: CELLS → TISSUES → ORGANS → ORGAN SYSTEMS → ORGANISM. Each level builds on the previous one.

wrongExplains.0: Tissues come before organs, not after.
wrongExplains.2: The sequence goes from simple to complex, not reverse.
wrongExplains.3: Cells come before tissues, not after.

scaffolds.q: The heart is made of muscle tissue, connective tissue, epithelial tissue, and nervous tissue. What level is the heart?
scaffolds.explain: The heart is an ORGAN because it contains MULTIPLE TISSUE TYPES working together for a specific function (pumping blood).

wrongExplains.0: A tissue is one cell type; the heart has multiple tissue types.
wrongExplains.2: A cell is the smallest living unit; the heart contains millions.
wrongExplains.3: A molecule is a chemical unit; the heart is an anatomical structure.

scaffolds.q: A structure composed of two or more tissue types working together is called a(n) ___?
scaffolds.explain: The answer is ORGAN. Not a tissue (single cell type), not a cell, not an organ system (multiple organs). An organ = 2+ tissue types, specific function.

wrongExplains.0: A tissue is one cell type; organs have multiple tissue types.
wrongExplains.2: A cell is simpler than an organ; organs contain many cells.
wrongExplains.3: An organ system is multiple organs working together.
